mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it ideal for organizations looking to implement AI solutions efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step installs Kagenti using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources used.

```bash

echo 'Monitor your Google Cloud billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

```

> This is a reminder to keep track of your cloud expenses.
commands: echo 'Monitor your Google Cloud billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Note that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster and its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current deployment. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Check the status of the current pods.
> Upgrades the Kagenti installation.
> Verify that the new pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti Helm repository may not be added correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti' to add the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud resources before starting the installation.
